label: Creation – Goodness as Baseline
description: God creates a very good world and gives humanity image-bearing vocation under His rule.
identity: God is the good, wise Creator who brings ordered life by His word.
enactment: God speaks, creates, blesses, and gives humanity vocation and limits in the garden.
effect: Creation is very good; humanity bears God's image and receives delegated rule under God's authority.

label: The Fall – Distrust and Fracture
description: Human distrust of God's word brings shame, curse, and exile, yet with a promise of future victory.
identity: God is truthful, just, and the giver of life whose word defines reality.
enactment: The serpent lies, humans distrust God, grasp autonomy, and transgress His command.
effect: Shame, broken fellowship, curse, and exile enter history, along with the promise of the woman's seed.

label: The Flood – Judgment and Remnant
description: God judges a violent world yet preserves a remnant and sets a covenant sign.
identity: God is grieved by evil yet righteous and faithful to His purposes.
enactment: Humanity fills the earth with violence; God sends the flood yet preserves Noah and his family.
effect: The world is purged, a remnant is saved, and God establishes a covenant with a rainbow as the sign.

label: Call of Abram – Blessed to Bless
description: God calls Abram and ties global blessing to His promise and Abram's trusting obedience.
identity: God is sovereign and missional, the One who blesses in order to bless the nations.
enactment: God calls Abram to go from his country and promises blessing; Abram responds in faith and goes.
effect: The covenant line begins and the nations are destined to be blessed through Abram's seed.

label: Binding of Isaac – Tested Trust and Provision
description: Abraham's obedience at the edge of promise reveals God as provider and confirms the covenant.
identity: God is the tester and provider who will Himself provide the sacrificial lamb.
enactment: God commands Abraham to offer Isaac; Abraham obeys in faith up to the point of sacrifice.
effect: God provides a substitute, confirms His promise with an oath, and sets a pattern of substitutionary sacrifice.

label: Exodus – Salvation by Mighty Act
description: God delivers Israel from Egypt through judgment and redemption, revealing Himself as covenant deliverer.
identity: God is I AM, the covenant-keeping deliverer and judge of idols.
enactment: God sends plagues, institutes Passover, and splits the sea to bring Israel out of slavery.
effect: Israel is freed, Egypt is humbled, and God is known as saviour-judge among the nations.

label: Sinai Covenant – Law, Failure, and Renewed Presence
description: God binds Himself to Israel with law and presence, then renews the covenant after their failure.
identity: God is holy, near, and merciful, abounding in steadfast love yet not clearing the guilty.
enactment: God gives Torah and Israel vows obedience but commits idolatry with the golden calf.
effect: The covenant is renewed with mediated mercy; God's presence remains with conditions through the tabernacle.

label: Sacrificial System – Sin, Substitution, Access
description: God provides a sacrificial system where blood atones and sin is borne away, granting conditional access.
identity: God is holy yet provides a way for atonement and ongoing fellowship.
enactment: Priests offer sacrifices and the high priest performs atonement rites on the Day of Atonement.
effect: Sin is ritually covered and sent away, enabling covenant access while pointing beyond itself to a greater sacrifice.

label: Deuteronomic Hinge – Life and Death Set Before You
description: Blessing and curse in the land hinge on Israel's covenant obedience, while a future heart work is promised.
identity: God is covenantally faithful, blessing obedience and bringing curse for persistent treachery.
enactment: Israel is summoned to love and obey God from the heart and warned against idolatry and disobedience.
effect: Blessing or exile follows their response, and a future promise of heart-circumcision anticipates deeper restoration.

label: Davidic Covenant – Kingdom Seed
description: God ties His purposes to a royal son whose throne will endure forever despite human failure.
identity: God is the king-maker whose kingdom plan is anchored in His promise, not human performance alone.
enactment: God promises David a house, a throne, and a son who will be God's son and rule the nations.
effect: The Davidic line becomes the locus of hope; later collapse intensifies longing for the true Messiah.

label: Prophetic Indictment and Hope – Justice and New Heart
description: Prophets announce judgment for injustice and idolatry yet promise a new covenant, Spirit, and restoration.
identity: God is just and compassionate, jealous for His name and determined to restore a people for Himself.
enactment: God confronts sin through the prophets, sends His people into exile, and promises a new heart and Spirit.
effect: Exile and remnant purification prepare for a future restoration with law written on hearts and Spirit-enabled obedience.

label: Incarnation – Word Made Flesh
description: The eternal Word becomes flesh, revealing God and taking the path of humiliation toward the cross.
identity: The Son is the eternal Word, true image of God, equal with the Father.
enactment: The Son takes on human nature, dwells among us, and humbles Himself to serve and suffer.
effect: God is made known in the face of Jesus Christ and the way is opened for atoning death and exaltation.

label: The Cross – Judgment and Justification Hinge
description: At the cross God judges sin in Christ and justifies those who trust Him, uniting justice and mercy.
identity: God is just and the justifier of the one who has faith in Jesus.
enactment: Christ offers Himself as a once-for-all atoning sacrifice, bearing the wrath and curse due to sinners.
effect: Sins are forgiven, righteousness is granted by faith, and a new covenant people is formed in Christ.

label: Resurrection – New Creation Launched
description: God raises Jesus bodily from the dead, launching new creation and securing our future resurrection.
identity: God is the life-giver who will not abandon His Holy One to the grave.
enactment: God raises Jesus from the dead and seats Him at His right hand as Lord.
effect: Jesus becomes the firstfruits of the resurrection; death is defeated and believers' future resurrection is guaranteed.

label: Pentecost – Spirit Poured Out
description: The exalted Christ pours out the Spirit on His people, forming the church and empowering mission.
identity: God indwells His people by the Spirit as the seal and firstfruits of the age to come.
enactment: The risen Christ pours out the Holy Spirit on Jews and later Gentiles who trust Him.
effect: The church is born as a multi-ethnic Spirit-filled community, empowered for witness with law written on hearts.

label: Justification by Faith – New Covenant Center
description: God justifies the ungodly by grace through faith in Christ apart from works of the law.
identity: God is the gracious justifier who saves not by human works but by union with Christ.
enactment: People trust in Christ rather than in law-keeping or works for righteousness before God.
effect: They receive a new status of righteousness, peace with God, and access into grace in which they stand.

label: Union with Christ – New Identity and Life
description: Believers are united to Christ in His death and resurrection, receiving a new identity and power to live.
identity: Christ is the new Adam and believers are in Him, no longer under condemnation.
enactment: Believers are counted as having died and risen with Christ and are called to consider themselves dead to sin and alive to God.
effect: A new way of life emerges, marked by progressive sanctification in the Spirit and freedom from sin's dominion.

label: One New Humanity – Church as Temple
description: In Christ God reconciles Jew and Gentile into one new humanity and dwelling place of God.
identity: God is the reconciler who creates peace through the blood of the cross.
enactment: Christ breaks down the dividing wall of hostility and preaches peace to those far and near.
effect: A multi-ethnic church is formed as God's temple, displaying His wisdom to the powers and principalities.

label: Perseverance and Apostasy Warnings
description: God uses real warnings and promises to keep His people persevering in faith.
identity: God is faithful and truthful; His warnings and promises are means He uses to guard His people.
enactment: Believers either hold fast to Christ in faith and obedience or drift, harden, and refuse to repent.
effect: Perseverance leads to rest and reward, while persistent unbelief leads to fearful judgment; the warnings function to secure the saints' perseverance.

label: Final Judgment and New Creation
description: Christ returns to judge the living and the dead and to renew creation.
identity: God is the righteous judge and faithful bridegroom who makes all things new.
enactment: Christ sits in judgment, separates sheep from goats, and brings in the new heavens and new earth.
effect: The righteous enjoy eternal life in God's renewed creation, while the unrepentant face exclusion and judgment; God dwells with His people forever.
